title: Gothofreda suaveolens
taxonomy: Gothofreda suaveolens is classified as a member of the family Amaryllidaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. It belongs to the genus Gothofreda, which is a small genus of flowering plants. Gothofreda suaveolens is native to South Africa and is often found growing in rocky outcrops and mountainous regions. The classification of Gothofreda suaveolens is further divided into the subfamily Allioideae and the tribe Gilliesieae. Further research is necessary to determine the specific classification within this group.

Its roots are used to treat various health issues, including fever, and its leaves are valued for their anti-inflammatory properties.
